title: eria extinctoria
side_effects: Eria extinctoria can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Prolonged consumption of the plant's extracts can lead to digestive issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. In severe cases, improper use of Eria extinctoria can cause cardiovascular problems, including rapid heartbeat and blood pressure fluctuations. Additionally, the plant's toxic compounds can also affect the nervous system, resulting in headaches, dizziness, and seizures.
